lloyds_fy_results_2025.title: Lloyd’s market delivers strong full year performance; very strong balance sheet; increased capital
lloyds_fy_results_2025.summary: The Lloyd’s market reported a strong financial performance for the full year 2025, with a profit before tax of £10.6bn (+10.1%) and gross written premium of £57.9bn (+4.2%). Underpinning these results is a significantly strengthened balance sheet, with central solvency reaching 496%. Alongside the results, Lloyd’s launched a new five-year strategy aimed at sharpening the market's financial edge and maximizing its capital advantage.

crystal.title: Crystal+

fire_department_officials.summary: Emergency services responsible for fire safety and prevention, conducting inspections to ensure compliance with fire codes. ai_agent.title: AI Agent
ai_agent.summary: An AI agent is an autonomous software programme or system that perceives its environment, makes decisions, and takes actions to achieve specific goals, often leveraging large language models for complex reasoning and interaction. They are designed to automate tasks and workflows. The type of application or system that directly benefits from the token optimisation techniques discussed, leading to more cost-effective and efficient operations.

gemini.title: Gemini
gemini.summary: A family of multimodal large language models developed by Google AI, designed for advanced reasoning and understanding across different data types. One of the Large Language Models (LLMs) explicitly supported by the CrewAI framework for building AI agents. - Gemini is a compatible LLM for AI agents built with CrewAI.

autogen.title: AutoGen
autogen.summary: An open-source framework developed by Microsoft for creating multi-agent applications, featuring a scalable, distributed network of agents with asynchronous messaging. Presented as a popular AI agent framework capable of building multi-agent systems, suitable for various applications. - AutoGen includes AgentChat for conversational teams and AutoGen Studio for a no-code interface.

The provider of the core AI models (Gemini series) and ecosystem chosen for the memex project, primarily due to their large context window capabilities essential for knowledge synthesis.

semantic_kernel.title: Semantic Kernel
semantic_kernel.summary: An open-source SDK from Microsoft for building enterprise-grade Generative AI applications, providing abstractions for chat completion and assistant agents. Functions as both an agent framework and a process framework, enabling the orchestration of multiple agents through group chats or complex data-flow processes. - Semantic Kernel facilitates the building of enterprise-grade GenAI applications through its agent and process frameworks.

memex.title: memex
memex.summary: A personal AI-maintained second brain system being developed by the author to address information overload and fragmented knowledge. It aims to ingest, cross-link, and synthesise information, functioning more like a research librarian than a simple retrieval system. The central product and project being built by the author, inspired by Vannevar Bush's original vision, to create an advanced knowledge management system.

lawyer_companys.title: Lawyer (Company's)
lawyer_companys.summary: Legal counsel representing the IT worker's company. Advised on the lease agreement and is pursuing legal action against the landlord for damages. - Believes the company has a strong case for compensation against the landlord due to the unsafe building conditions.

graphify.title: Graphify
graphify.summary: Graphify is an open-source tool and AI agent skill that transforms files into a queryable knowledge graph. It is designed to solve "agent amnesia" and "context bloat" by providing a structured, compressed representation of a project. Directly relevant to the memex project's architecture, as it follows the llm_wiki_pattern and uses obsidian for persistence.

s_p_global.title: S&P Global
s_p_global.summary: S&P Global is a leading provider of transparent and independent ratings for the global capital markets. Their ratings offer essential insights into risk and opportunity. S&P Global is among the rating agencies that recognise Lloyd's strong financial position, reinforcing confidence among investors and policyholders.

python_3_12.title: Python 3.12
python_3_12.summary: A recent version of Python, a widely-used, high-level programming language known for its clear syntax and extensive libraries, making it popular for automation, web development, and AI. The programming language used to develop key automation scripts (ingest.py, lint.py, synthesise.py) that manage and process information within the memex system. - Powers the automation scripts that manage ingestion, linting, and synthesis processes for memex.

it_workers_company.title: IT Worker's Company
it_workers_company.summary: The employer of the narrator, which planned to establish a new office location. Suffered costs and delays due to the unsafe building conditions and is now seeking compensation from the landlord. - Is asking for compensation for the time, effort, and costs incurred during the entire situation.

Presented as a central theme in the Mahabharata, in constant interplay and tension with destiny, shaping the moral complexities and decisions of characters within the epic.

major_claims_ratio.title: Major Claims Ratio
major_claims_ratio.summary: The major claims ratio is the proportion of earned premiums used to cover significant, infrequent losses, such as those arising from large catastrophes. It indicates the impact of exceptional events on profitability. This ratio helps assess the volatility of an insurer's claims experience, distinguishing between routine losses and those from large-scale events that can significantly impact the underwriting result.

frictional_costs.summary:
Expenses incurred during the process of placing or transacting insurance, beyond the actual premium, encompassing administrative overhead, brokerage fees, and time spent on manual processes. lease_agreement.summary: A legally binding contractual arrangement calling for the lessee (tenant) to pay the lessor (landlord) for the use of an asset, such as a property. isolated_memory_ai.title: Isolated Memory (AI)
isolated_memory_ai.summary: A state where different AI tools or agents within a system do not share learned information, rules, or past interaction context with each other. Each tool maintains its own separate, unshared memory. This is the fundamental problem that Agentic Stack seeks to resolve.

fire_sprinkler_system.title: Fire Sprinkler System
fire_sprinkler_system.summary: An active fire protection method comprising a network of pipes and sprinkler heads designed to automatically detect and suppress fires by spraying water, thus controlling or extinguishing them. A critical life safety feature in commercial buildings, vital for protecting occupants and property from the devastating effects of fire. A non-functional system poses an extreme and unacceptable risk.

specialty_insurance.summary:

declarative_memory.title: Declarative Memory
declarative_memory.summary: Declarative memory in the context of AI agents refers to the explicit storage of facts, rules, and structures that an agent can query and reason over. This is distinct from the implicit weights of the model or the transient history of a single session. A critical component for avoiding "agent amnesia." Tools like graphify (via knowledge graphs) and agentic_stack (via the `.agent` folder) implement declarative memory to provide persistence across sessions and tools.

ai_agent_skills.summary: Extensible capabilities that can be added to an AI agent (like claude_code or gemini_cli) to perform specialized tasks beyond text generation, such as executing code, browsing the web, or generating diagrams. Poovi's workflow relies on highly capable agents. Understanding how to expand an agent's "toolbox" with skills like graphify or fireworks_tech_graph is key to his AI operations.

underlying_combined_ratio.summary: The underlying combined ratio is the combined ratio excluding the impact of major claims, providing a clearer view of the ongoing profitability from an insurer's regular business activities. This metric is important for evaluating the core, fundamental underwriting performance and resilience of the business, as it removes the distorting effects of volatile large losses.
